title: quassia grandifolia
intro: Quassia grandifolia, also known as the yellow dogwood or bitterwood, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its bitter compounds and quassin, a lignan that has been shown to have antiparasitic and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Quassia grandifolia include its potential use in treating fever, diarrhea, and intestinal worms, as well as its possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Quassia grandifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
